2604.26597.title: Translating Under Pressure: Domain-Aware LLMs for Crisis Communication
2604.26597.paper_summary: Timely and reliable multilingual communication is critical during natural and human-induced disasters, but developing effective solutions for crisis communication is limited by the scarcity of curated parallel data. We propose a domain-adaptive pipeline that expands a small reference corpus, by retrieving and filtering data from general corpora. We use the resulting dataset to fine-tune a small language model for crisis-domain translation and then apply preference optimization to bias outputs toward CEFR A2-level English. Automatic and human evaluation shows that this approach improves readability, while maintaining strong adequacy. Our results indicate that simplified English, combined with domain adaptation, can function as a practical lingua franca for emergency communication when full multilingual coverage is not feasible.
2604.26597.paper_summary_zh: 及時且可靠的多語言溝通在自然災害和人為災害期間至關重要，但由於精心策劃的平行數據稀缺，開發有效的危機溝通解決方案受到限制。<lb/>我們提出了一個領域自適應流程，通過從通用語料庫檢索和過濾數據來擴展一個小型參考語料庫。<lb/>我們使用生成的數據集來微調一個小型語言模型，以進行危機領域翻譯，然後應用偏好優化將輸出偏向CEFR A2級別的英語。<lb/>自動和人工評估顯示，這種方法提高了可讀性，同時保持了強大的充分性。<lb/>我們的結果表明，簡化英語結合領域適應可以作為在無法實現全面多語言覆蓋時的緊急溝通實用通用語。
2604.26597.author: Antonio Castaldo et.al.
2604.26597.authors: Antonio Castaldo, Maria Carmen Staiano, Johanna Monti, Sheila Castilho, Francesca Chiusaroli
